vocabulary.definition: dirty and in need of a wash
vocabulary.example: It was a tiny, grubby looking pub.
In fact, Harry had the most peculiar feeling that only he and Hagrid could see it.

vocabulary.definition: worn out and in poor condition
vocabulary.example: For a famous place, it was very dark and shabby.

vocabulary.definition: a small, clever magical creature that runs Gringotts bank
vocabulary.example: “Yeah, that’s a goblin,” said Hagrid quietly as they walked up the white stone steps toward him.

vocabulary.definition: a very large amount of money
vocabulary.example: And all the time there had been a small fortune belonging to him, buried deep under London.

vocabulary.definition: able to fold up into a smaller size
vocabulary.example: Cauldrons—All Sizes—Copper, Brass, Pewter, Silver—Self Stirring—Collapsible, said a sign hanging over them.

vocabulary.definition: strange or interesting in an unexpected way
vocabulary.example: He put Harry’s wand back into its box and wrapped it in brown paper, still muttering, “Curious… curious…”

vocabulary.definition: to twist and move with small quick movements
vocabulary.example: The brick he had touched quivered—it wriggled—in the middle, a small hole appeared—it grew wider and wider—a second later they were facing an archway large enough even for Hagrid, an archway onto a cobbled street that twisted and turned out of sight.

vocabulary.definition: to speak with pauses and repeated sounds, often from nervousness
vocabulary.example: He couldn’t stop stammering his thanks, sounding just like Professor Quirrell.
Told yeh, didn’t I? Told yeh you was famous.

vocabulary.definition: to smile or speak in a mean, mocking way
vocabulary.example: “Do you?” said the boy, with a slight sneer.

vocabulary.definition: magic spells that give objects special powers
vocabulary.example: “Spells—enchantments,” said Hagrid, unfolding his newspaper as he spoke.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Wakes to find the wizarding world is real and spends his first day shopping for school.

characters.name: Rubeus Hagrid
characters.description: Takes Harry to Gringotts, Diagon Alley, and every shop on the Hogwarts list.

characters.name: Draco Malfoy
characters.description: A pale, sneering boy in Madam Malkin's who looks down on Muggle-born wizards.

characters.name: Mr. Ollivander
characters.description: The wand-maker who finds Harry's holly and phoenix feather wand.

background.hook: Harry's magical birthday continues with a trip to London. From the hidden Leaky Cauldron to the goblin-run bank at Gringotts and the wonders of Diagon Alley, he discovers he is rich, famous — and about to get a wand that shares a strange connection with his scar.
background.readingFocus: Notice how wizards react when they recognize Harry. Watch what he buys and what the pale boy in the robe shop says about blood and family.

miniCheck.stem: At Gringotts, the bank is run by —

options.text: a very large amount of money

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Hagrid takes Harry to Diagon Alley to get money, school supplies, an owl, and a wand
Harry boards the Hogwarts Express for the first time
Hagrid tells Harry he is a wizard
Harry receives his first Hogwarts letter in the mail

comprehension.explanation: The chapter follows Harry's shopping trip from the Leaky Cauldron through Gringotts and every Diagon Alley shop to Ollivander's.

comprehension.stem: How do Harry and Hagrid enter Diagon Alley?

options.text: Hagrid taps bricks in the courtyard wall and it opens into an archway
They fly on broomsticks over London
A goblin escorts them through the Underground
They walk through the front door of Gringotts

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid counts bricks above the trash can, taps the wall three times, and a hole grows into an archway onto Diagon Alley.

comprehension.stem: What does Harry find in his vault at Gringotts?

options.text: Mounds of gold Galleons, silver Sickles, and bronze Knuts
An empty vault with nothing inside

comprehension.explanation: When the green smoke clears, Harry gasps at columns of gold, silver, and heaps of bronze Knuts.

comprehension.stem: How do people in the Leaky Cauldron react when they see Harry?

options.text: They are thrilled and crowd around to shake his hand
They call the Ministry of Magic
They ask him to leave immediately

comprehension.explanation: The pub goes still, then everyone wants to meet Harry Potter and shake his hand.

comprehension.stem: Why does Mr. Ollivander call Harry's wand situation curious?

options.text: The phoenix feather in Harry's wand came from the same bird as the wand that gave him his scar
Harry chose the wrong wand on purpose
Harry's wand is made of rubber
No one has ever bought a wand before

comprehension.explanation: Ollivander says the phoenix gave only one other feather — to the wand that scarred Harry.

comprehension.stem: What pet does Hagrid buy for Harry?
comprehension.explanation: They leave Eeylops Owl Emporium with a beautiful snowy owl asleep in a cage.

comprehension.stem: Why does Harry like Hagrid less after meeting the boy in Madam Malkin's?

options.text: The boy insults Hagrid, calling him a savage servant who sets fire to his bed
Hagrid refuses to buy Harry a broomstick
The boy is Harry's cousin Dudley
Hagrid agrees with the boy about Muggle families

comprehension.explanation: The pale boy sneers that Hagrid is a savage gamekeeper; Harry says coldly that he thinks Hagrid is brilliant.

vocabulary.explanation: The Leaky Cauldron is described as a tiny, grubby looking pub.

vocabulary.stem: "Yeah, that's a ___," said Hagrid quietly as they walked up the white stone steps toward him.
vocabulary.explanation: A goblin in scarlet and gold stands by Gringotts' bronze doors.

options.text: a magic spell on an object

vocabulary.explanation: Hagrid says spells and enchantments protect Gringotts from robbers.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'shabby'?
vocabulary.explanation: Despite being famous, the Leaky Cauldron inside is dark and shabby.

vocabulary.stem: And all the time there had been a small ___ belonging to him, buried deep under London.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry realizes the Dursleys never knew about the fortune waiting for him at Gringotts.

cloze.title: Diagon Alley
paragraphs: Hagrid led Harry to the (1) ___ looking Leaky Cauldron, where Harry had a (2) ___ feeling that only they could see the pub. Inside, the famous place was dark and (3) ___.

At (4) ___ bank, Harry discovered a small (5) ___ in gold and silver. In Diagon Alley they bought books, a snowy owl, and at Ollivander's a wand that made red and gold sparks — though Mr. Ollivander found it very (6) ___.
To reach the alley, Hagrid tapped the courtyard wall until a brick (7) ___ and opened into a twisting cobbled street.

gaps.explanation: The Leaky Cauldron is a tiny, grubby looking pub.
Harry has the most peculiar feeling that only he and Hagrid can see the pub.
For a famous place, the Leaky Cauldron is very dark and shabby inside.
Gringotts is run by goblins and is the wizards' bank.
Harry finds a small fortune belonging to him buried deep under London.
Ollivander mutters "Curious… curious…" about Harry's wand.
The brick Hagrid touched quivered and wriggled until an archway opened.
